text:
Once upon a time in the vibrant town of Flowerfield, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Every dawn, Oliver would perch atop a ancient oak overlooking the meadow. One day, a little robin named Ruby noticed Oliver's wisdom glazed over his bright eyes.

"Oh wise owl," Ruby chirped. "What's out there so special?"

With a gentle flap of his wings, Oliver began a story.

"There once were colorful flowers that sang melodies so sweet. Each bloom would sing a different tune, and the birds would sway to their music. One day, a storm blew through Flowerfield. Rain poured down as dark clouds obscured the sun. Through the darkness, the singing stopped. But the flowers didn't mind. They rested, drying themselves out under the stars.

"The stars sang to them instead. And suddenly, new songs sprang to life. The roses sang of love lost forever, the violets sighed sweet goodbye.

"Just as dreams can't be stolen, the flowers thought
